articles.title: Iran war delays delivery of medical goods to other parts of the world
articles.summary: The war in Iran has slowed down international shipping, much of which contains medical and humanitarian goods destined for Asia and Africa.

articles.title: Iran hits oil tanker off Dubai as fighting continues on all fronts
articles.summary: Iran attacked and set on fire a massive Kuwaiti oil tanker off Dubai overnight, as Gulf states increasingly suffer the fallout from the war.

articles.title: Deaths in ICE Custody Are Growing, and Trump Defends Ballroom Plans
articles.summary: Plus, a three-minute, multimillion-dollar art heist.

articles.title: China's AI chatbots are advanced and versatile — and begging for more users
articles.summary: Chinese AI companies are focused less on being cutting edge and more on attracting customers. That means holiday promotions, and making chatbots useful in everyday life.

articles.title: In a town close to the farmworker movement, some struggle to process Chavez allegations
articles.summary: March 31 is Cesar Chavez's birthday, and a longtime holiday. In the wake of sexual assault allegations against him, residents in the farming town of Delano are conflicted about how to remember him.

articles.title: Trump Faces a Decision on Whether to Start a Ground War in Iran
articles.summary: The president wants a negotiation, but the Iranians say they are refusing until a cease-fire is declared. And while Marines and the 82nd Airborne Division offer new leverage, the risks escalate quickl

articles.title: Drivers Count Their Pennies As Gas Hits $4 a Gallon
articles.summary: Since the Iran conflict began on Feb. 28, gas prices across the United States have increased about 35 percent. They are now above $4 a gallon, and drivers are wincing.

articles.title: Europe Has a ‘Guns vs. Butter’ Problem. War in Iran Makes It Worse.
articles.summary: After decades of prioritizing domestic over military spending, the continent’s leaders are trying to pivot. That is straining national budgets and could anger voters.

articles.title: The final batch of World Cup tickets is about to go on sale. Here are 5 things to know
articles.summary: FIFA is kicking off its last sales for World Cup tickets on Wednesday. From prices to why FOMO is working against you, here's what you need to know.

articles.title: Babies are an afterthought in the birthright citizenship case, advocates say
articles.summary: An end to birthright citizenship would mean a new layer of bureaucracy for all babies born in the U.S. and could cause delays for health insurance and other benefits.

articles.title: How Trump's EEOC is attacking DEI and emphasizing white people
articles.summary: Andrea Lucas, the Trump-appointed chair of the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, has set a new agenda for an agency that long prioritized vulnerable and underserved workers.

articles.title: Morning news brief
articles.summary: Iran opens Strait of Hormuz to some ships as Gulf countries face strikes, Trump's mixed messages on the war in Iran could hurt the GOP, TSA officers are receiving back pay as DHS remains unfunded.

articles.title: 'We call it the walking bus': How kids are getting to school amid ICE operations
articles.summary: In cities where ICE operations have surged, community members are walking kids to school.

articles.title: Russia is building in parts of Ukraine. What does it mean for a peace deal?
articles.summary: Russia is heavily developing occupied territory in Ukraine, suggesting it has no plans to return the land as part of any peace deal. NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Reuters reporter Mari Saito.

articles.title: Offensive vs. Defensive: On Iran, the Distinction Matters for Britain’s Leader
articles.summary: As the United States expands its armada of warplanes on British soil, Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he is trying to defend Britain’s interests while keeping the country out of war.

articles.title: U.S. Gas Prices Hit $4 a Gallon on Average
articles.summary: A month since the first U.S.-Israeli attacks and Iran’s response effectively shut off Persian Gulf oil, drivers are paying significantly more to fill up.

articles.title: Fresh Food Distributors Add Surcharges as Fuel Costs Rise
articles.summary: Delivering salmon, fruits and other perishable foods has become more expensive as the war with Iran pushes up diesel prices.

articles.title: Army reviewing after helicopters hovered alongside Kid Rock's swimming pool as he saluted
articles.summary: The Army has launched an administrative review after two AH-64 Apache helicopters on a training run hovered near the hillside home of Kid Rock as the outspoken supporter of President Donald Trump salu

articles.title: Iran’s Fractured Leadership Is Struggling to Coordinate, Officials Say
articles.summary: As leaders are killed and replaced, Iranian negotiators may not know what their government is willing to concede in any negotiations.

articles.title: Figure skating season ends with redemption and heartbreak. What do fans watch next?
articles.summary: Worlds marks the last competition of the 2025-2026 season. Skaters have some time to go on tour, rest up and learn new routines before the next season starts in July.

articles.title: TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon
articles.summary: TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.

articles.title: Iran's strike wounded over a dozen U.S. personnel and hit valuable jets in Saudi Arabia
articles.summary: NPR has confirmed that at least two U.S. E-3 Sentry aircraft were damaged and more than a dozen U.S. service members were injured in an Iranian missile and drone attack in Saudi Arabia on Friday.

articles.title: A skeleton discovered in a Dutch church may belong to musketeer d'Artagnan
articles.summary: Human remains found in a church in the Netherlands could be those of d'Artagnan, one of the legendary French swordsmen who inspired the novel The Three Musketeers.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
